feedback.text: I do think the addition of monetary gambling in video games causes harm to children and for example depressed and neurodivergent people who can not foresee the consequences of their actions. I do think there should be heavier regulation in gambling or gambling like games (video games, slot machines etc.) in the EU to protect vulnerable people who might end up in poverty because of gambling. Fear of missing out features should be stated more openly. It would influence marketing and web-store sales but also other limited-quantity items or products that use scarcity to increace prices unethically. Again it makes vulnerable people to be in danger because they are at higher risk of unnecessary purchases. Video games, movies, comics, books etc. should not be sold as licenses but rather products to keep. If you buy a digital version of a book, there should be some plan on how you can access the book even if the provider goes bankrupt. Ie. there should be a download locally option or in case of software the source code should be given out to community. It would also be beneficial for competition when other companies could improve old software or learn from it. I do think after COVID19 the digital censorship has increased quite a bit especially since 2024-2025. There should be less limitations on free speech and users privacy should be respected. There should not be face-id scans on internet to access sites or the sites should be specified (ie. banking) for security reasons that protect the customer.
